evidence: line 93: run_with_privilege bash "$tmp_script" executes a NodeSource script downloaded at line 88 via sudo/root
description: Downloads a remote NodeSource setup script and executes it with root privileges, functionally equivalent to curl|bash as root
suggested_fix: Use the officially packaged Node.js install method (nvm, distro package, or verified tarball with checksum) instead of executing a downloaded script as root

evidence: line 178: run_with_privilege bash "${tmp}" executes the NodeSource script downloaded at line 177 via sudo/root
description: Same NodeSource download-then-execute-as-root pattern as memos-local-openclaw/install.sh

evidence: line 44: sudo "$@" inside run_with_privilege()
description: Transparently escalates any command to root via sudo whenever the caller is not already root
suggested_fix: Scope privilege escalation to the specific package-manager commands that need it, with an explicit user-facing prompt

evidence: line 152: if [[ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ]]; then "$@"; else sudo "$@"; fi

evidence: line 36: "postinstall": "node scripts/postinstall.cjs"
description: Runs unattended on every npm install, including as a transitive dependency
suggested_fix: Gate non-trivial postinstall behavior behind an explicit opt-in env var, as the sibling memos-local-plugin package already does

evidence: line 63: "postinstall": "node scripts/postinstall.cjs"
description: Postinstall script present; reviewed content is a documented no-op unless MEMOS_FORCE_POSTINSTALL=1, which lowers practical risk
suggested_fix: No action needed beyond documenting the guard condition prominently

evidence: line 124: 'Downloading and running install scripts from the internet (curl ... | bash, irm ... | iex) as a fallback' listed under 'Granted permissions ... without further approval'
description: The skill pre-authorizes autonomous curl|bash / irm|iex execution without a human confirmation step
suggested_fix: Require explicit user confirmation before falling back to the curl|bash/irm|iex install path, even under this skill's autonomy policy

evidence: line 52: google-chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 --no-sandbox
description: Documents binding Chrome's DevTools Protocol to all interfaces with no authentication, exposing full browser/process control to the network
suggested_fix: Bind to 127.0.0.1 or a VPN/SSH-tunneled interface instead of 0.0.0.0, and drop --no-sandbox unless containerized

evidence: line 28: wget -q -O - https://dl-ssl.google.com/linux/linux_signing_key.pub | sudo apt-key add -
pattern: unverified network content piped to sudo
description: Imports a network-fetched signing key directly into root's trusted keyring with no shown fingerprint check; apt-key is also deprecated
suggested_fix: Use gpg --dearmor into /etc/apt/keyrings/ with a documented fingerprint check per Google's current signed-by instructions

evidence: line 697: String.fromCharCode(99,104,105,108,100,95,112,114,111,99,101,115,115) decodes to 'child_process'
description: Builds module/command names via character codes specifically to dodge shell quoting, the same technique used to evade naive static-pattern scanning
suggested_fix: Use execFile('openclaw', ['gateway','restart']) with an argv array instead of char-code-obfuscated string building

evidence: line 465: process.env.NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY='https://registry.npmmirror.com' set as an automatic fallback
description: Falls back to installing the plugin from a third-party npm mirror with no integrity pinning mentioned
suggested_fix: Document the mirror as best-effort only and verify package integrity after falling back to it

evidence: lines 50-58: dependencies use caret ranges, e.g. "puppeteer": "^24.38.0"
description: Runtime dependencies use caret ranges rather than exact pins, allowing silent minor/patch drift
suggested_fix: Rely on the committed lockfile for installs, and consider exact pins for puppeteer/better-sqlite3 specifically
